```{r} library(tidyverse) library(tidyselect) library(tibble) library(sf) library(tigris) library(lubridate) library(tidygeocoder) library(gt) library(gtsummary) # nice count visualizations library(forcats) # a specific data wrangling issue library(readxl) ``` THIS IS STILL A MAJOR WORK IN PROGRESS. I JUST HAVE THE RAW FORMAT HERE. # The United States ```{r, output = FALSE} # data that I type in manually daniel_travel_map <- readxl::read_xlsx("trip_data.xlsx") # I can also add coordinate information here... maybe play around with that? # US states shapefile us <- states(cb = TRUE) |> shift_geometry() #for alaska and hawaii (and PR) ``` These visualizations do not take into account smaller trips within the state I live in (e.g. travelling from Allston to Boston doesn't count, but something like Haverhill to Fall River counts). These are just ones I thought were memorable. Thus, I don't count just passing through a state. I also don't count states I have lived in; that will probably be a separate visualization. ```{r} # Count visit_counts <- daniel_travel_map |> count(state, name = "visits") # Join counts to map map_data <- us |> left_join( visit_counts, by=c("NAME" = "state") ) |> mutate( visits = ifelse(is.na(visits), 0, visits) ) # Plot ggplot(map_data) + geom_sf(aes(fill = visits)) + coord_sf(xlim = c(-3500000, 3500000), ylim = c(-2500000, 2500000), expand = FALSE) + #stupid fix by playing around with numbers scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Trips") + scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue") + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "How many times have I made significant travel to this state?") ``` 2. Timeline on unique states I have been to (manually add states I lived in like California), and on the X axis, show the state name. 3. Small multiples visualization of states I have visited each year + lived in (e.g. 2025 will have MA and NY as "lived" and RI, TX as travelled) 4. Word cloud of my travel descriptions 5. Descriptive statistics: how many redeyes, how many business vs leisure trips, distribution of days I stayed (faceted by something), average length of trip by days, number of day trips, average distance of trips (MAYBE) etc. 6. Capital city list: Have I been to the state capital?
